title: Nursery Rhyme. CCCCLIII. Love And Matrimony.
content:
Tommy Trot, a man of law,
        Sold his bed and lay upon straw:
        Sold the straw and slept on grass,
        To buy his wife a looking-glass.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Unknown, titled "Nursery Rhyme. CCCCLIII. Love And Matrimony.", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
